items.title: Loan approval (classification)
items.scenario: A bank trains a decision tree on 10k past applications with features: income, debt_ratio, credit_score, employment_years. Root split: credit_score ≤ 620 routes left; deeper splits on income and debt_ratio separate approve vs deny. A new applicant with credit_score = 580 and high debt_ratio lands in a leaf where 92% of training samples were denied — predict deny.
items.explanation: Axis-aligned rules mimic human underwriting checklists. Leaf majority vote gives a class label; following the path from root explains why.

items.title: Iris species (classic tabular demo)
items.scenario: Three-class iris dataset: petal_length ≤ 2.45 sends most samples to a Setosa leaf (pure). petal_width ≤ 1.75 further separates Versicolor vs Virginica. Entropy at the root is high (~1.58 bits for 3 balanced classes); after the first split, left child entropy drops to 0 (pure Setosa).
items.explanation: Shows impurity falling after informative splits. A pure leaf (one class only) has Gini = 0 and entropy = 0.

items.title: House price estimation (regression tree)
items.scenario: Features: sqft, bedrooms, zip_cluster. Splits on sqft ≤ 1800 send samples to a leaf predicting mean price $285k; sqft > 1800 with bedrooms ≤ 3 predicts $410k. A 1600 sqft home traverses to the $285k leaf.
items.explanation: Regression trees use variance reduction instead of Gini/entropy. Leaf value is the average y of training points in that region — piecewise constant approximation.

items.title: Customer churn with encoded categoricals
items.scenario: plan_type is one-hot encoded (basic, premium, enterprise). Tree splits on plan_type_premium ≤ 0.5 (i.e., not premium) combined with tenure_months ≤ 6 route high-churn customers to a leaf with 78% churn rate.
items.explanation: CART needs numeric columns — categoricals enter via encoding. Each one-hot column becomes a threshold split (≤ 0.5 vs > 0.5).

items.title: Overfitting on small data
items.scenario: Team trains max_depth=None on 200 samples and 30 features. Training accuracy hits 100% but validation accuracy is 62%. Setting max_depth=4 and min_samples_leaf=10 raises validation accuracy to 71%.
items.explanation: Unrestricted depth lets the tree create leaves for individual noise points. Complexity constraints align with the bias–variance sweet spot.
